pi_items.source: Task description step 5: 'Create a folder named 'JournalClub' in the Documents directory (~/Documents/JournalClub/)'
Task description step 1: 'Search for 'Circadian Rhythm'.' Verified as valid PubMed search term with extensive scientific literature (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/)
Task description step 3: 'Download the PDF full text for THREE (3) distinct articles.'

Task description step 4: 'Rename each PDF file using the format 'FirstAuthor_Year.pdf' (e.g., if the first author is Smith and published in 2023, name it 'Smith_2023.pdf'). The regex pattern correctly matches this format.
Official PubMed homepage: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/ (confirmed via web search)

As of October 2024, PubMed Central updated to new domain pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov (https://ncbiinsights.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/2024/10/21/pubmed-central-updated-website/). The old domain ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc still functions with redirects but is outdated.

privileged_info_summary: The PubMed Central Paper Archival task requires users to search for 'Circadian Rhythm' on PubMed (pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov), download three PDF articles with free full text access, and save them to ~/Documents/JournalClub/ with naming format FirstAuthor_Year.pdf. The data source is the real PubMed and PubMed Central databases containing peer-reviewed biomedical literature. One metadata domain is outdated: the current PubMed Central domain is pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov rather than ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc, though the old domain still functions via redirects.
